rag_rules: Place output range guarantees in component type annexes using only visible output features and concrete bounds from the requirement.
generic_rules: Use boolean helper declarations to encapsulate range conditions for clarity and type consistency.
Guarantees must reference only features exposed at the component type level, not implementation details.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Define range conditions as bool-typed equations: x >= min and x <= max; use them in guarantee clauses.
End AGREE annex blocks with }; after the last clause.
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing subcomponent ports or implementation-level variables in type-level annexes.
Do not use undefined identifiers or unbound literals; all values must derive from visible or declared symbols.
irrelevant_topics: AADL component implementation scope binding logic
